headline: Why The Huntington Ingalls Industries (HII) Story Is Shifting As Analysts Rework Targets And Risks
summary: Huntington Ingalls Industries is now modeled with a fair value price target of $403.00, up from $380.60, which brings the valuation closer to the latest analyst target range. That shift comes as research updates highlight both supportive factors, such as resolved union wage discussions and sector work pointing to higher targets, and cautious signals, including list removals and trimmed estimates tied to policy uncertainty. As you read on, you will see how these mixed opinions shape the...

headline: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
summary: The chair’s resignation letter was strongly worded, say the analysts, but they note that the lender has emphasized that it faces no known operational and governance issues and that its governance architecture remains intact.  While the insurer’s 2025 new business value was slightly below market expectations, growth in the nine new regions it expanded to in China was in line with its long-term growth compound annual growth rate target of 40% over 2025-30, he says in a note.  Its China business segment continues to be strong in the first two months of 2026, gaining more than 20% on year, he adds.

headline: Citi's surprise index shows longest upside run since financial crisis, awaits war impact
